title: AI contracts, not bitcoin, now drive miner valuations, and Cipher and TeraWulf look cheap, says analyst
content: Compass Point analysts Michael Donovan and Ed Engel said markets are giving little credit to future AI data center pipelines despite billions of dollars in signed leases.
summary: Compass Point analysts Michael Donovan and Ed Engel state that AI contracts, rather than Bitcoin, are now the primary drivers of miner valuations. They note that markets are currently undervaluing future AI data center pipelines despite billions in signed leases, suggesting that companies like Cipher and TeraWulf appear cheap.

title: Over $7.2 billion have migrated from LayerZero to Chainlink CCIP as Mantle joins exodus
content: Migrations to Chainlink CCIP so far include Kelp and Lombard, both of which brought over $1 billion, as well as Solv Protocol, Virtuals, Re and Kraken’s tokenized assets.
summary: Over $7.2 billion in assets have migrated from LayerZero to Chainlink's CCIP cross-chain interoperability protocol, with Mantle being the latest to join this significant shift. Other major migrations include Kelp and Lombard, both contributing over $1 billion each, alongside Solv Protocol, Virtuals, Re, and Kraken's tokenized assets.

title: Swift rolls out new blockchain ledger to bring 24/7 banking to 17 global giants
content: HSBC, UBS, Wells Fargo and Citi are among the 17 banks now preparing to pilot live transactions using tokenized digital assets on Swift’s new blockchain payments platform.
summary: Swift is rolling out a new blockchain ledger designed to enable 24/7 banking for global financial institutions. Major banks like HSBC, UBS, Wells Fargo, and Citi are among 17 giants preparing to pilot live transactions using tokenized digital assets on this new payments platform.

title: Singapore's Temasek investment fund says crypto is off the table, will focus on AI
content: The $400 billion wealth fund plans to expand AI holdings to 15% of its portfolio by 2031 from the current 6%.
summary: Singapore's $400 billion sovereign wealth fund, Temasek, has announced it will no longer invest in cryptocurrency. Instead, the fund plans to significantly increase its focus on Artificial Intelligence, aiming to expand AI holdings from 6% to 15% of its portfolio by 2031.

title: Anthropic, OpenAI, and SpaceX are bigger than the last 25 years of tech exits
content: Three big AI IPOs are set to generate more value than all the U.S. VC-backed exits since 2000.
summary: Three major AI companies, Anthropic, OpenAI, and SpaceX, are expected to generate more value than all U.S. VC-backed exits since 2000 through their upcoming IPOs. This milestone emphasizes the significant growth and impact of the AI industry. The combined value of these IPOs highlights the increasing importance of AI in the tech sector.

title: Bitcoin zips higher to nearly $64,000 as chip rally and yen strength drive gains
content: Bitcoin is up 4.2% over seven days that contained an oil shock, a bond selloff and two rounds of U.S. strikes on Iran.
summary: Bitcoin's price has increased to nearly $64,000, with a 4.2% gain over the past seven days, despite significant global events such as an oil shock and US strikes on Iran. The Rally was driven by a chip rally and yen strength. This increase is notable given the current global economic climate.

title: Arbitrum jumps 19% benefitting from Robinhood's $568 million onchain trading frenzy
content: The brokerage's new blockchain is off to a fast start, with memecoin trading boosting activity and revenue flowing back to the Arbitrum ecosystem.
summary: Arbitrum's token price jumped 19% following a $568 million on-chain trading frenzy on Robinhood's new blockchain. This significant activity, largely driven by memecoin trading, is generating revenue that flows back into the Arbitrum ecosystem.

title: Newest version of crypto Clarity Act may drop as soon as next week, sources say
content: After a long stretch without breakthroughs in the crypto market structure bill, insiders say a new draft may emerge in a final push for late-July action — though it still lacks bipartisan buy-in.
summary: A new draft of the crypto Clarity Act may be released as soon as next week, according to sources, in a final push for late-July action. The bill aims to address the crypto market structure, but still lacks bipartisan support. Its release is anticipated, but the outcome remains uncertain.

title: Billions flowing out of bitcoin ETFs and private credit funds suggest rising market risks
content: Redemption requests in the $2 trillion private credit market surged to $15.6 billion in the second quarter, dwarfing bitcoin ETF outflows.
summary: Redemption requests in private credit markets have surged, with $15.6 billion in outflows in the second quarter, exceeding outflows from bitcoin ETFs. This shift in investor behavior may indicate rising market risks. The significant outflows from both private credit and bitcoin ETFs suggest a broader trend of investors becoming more cautious.

title: Ethereum's newest nonprofit wants to become Wall Street's guide to crypto
content: In this week's edition of The Protocol Newsletter, we’re digging into Ethereum Institutional, a new nonprofit aimed at educating financial institutions and banks about Ethereum.
summary: Ethereum Institutional is a newly established nonprofit organization focused on educating financial institutions and banks about the Ethereum blockchain. This initiative aims to guide Wall Street into the world of cryptocurrency. The news was featured in The Protocol Newsletter.

title: Aave rolls out vaults for yield-hungry fintech investors
content: Aave's new Stable Vaults product lets wallets, exchanges and payment apps offer yields on stablecoin deposits
summary: Aave has introduced its new Stable Vaults product. This innovation allows fintech companies, including wallets, exchanges, and payment applications, to provide yields on stablecoin deposits to their users.

title: Latin America’s biggest stock exchange now offers options on bitcoin, ether and solana futures
content: These options settle into underlying futures contracts rather than spot cryptoassets, involving no custody, transfer, or administration of tokens.
summary: Latin America's largest stock exchange has launched options on futures contracts for Bitcoin, Ether, and Solana. These new financial instruments settle into underlying futures contracts, rather than spot cryptoassets, eliminating the need for token custody, transfer, or administration.

title: Crypto for Advisors: Q2 2026 Digital Asset Review
content: ETFs face record outflows
summary: The Q2 2026 Digital Asset Review, aimed at financial advisors, reported a significant development in the cryptocurrency market. During this period, exchange-traded funds (ETFs) experienced record outflows, indicating a notable shift in investment behavior.

title: Sony secures conditional approval to set up U.S. stablecoin trust bank
content: The New York-based subsidiary, fully owned by Sony Bank, will be capitalized with $40 million to support its upcoming stablecoin business operations.
summary: Sony has received conditional approval to establish a US-based trust bank for stablecoin operations, with a $40 million capitalization. The bank will be a subsidiary of Sony Bank and will support the company's stablecoin business. This move marks Sony's entry into the cryptocurrency market.

title: Two blockbuster public debut of AI stocks could pull away more capital from crypto
content: A wave of blockbuster AI listings is drawing billions of dollars from global investors, reinforcing a rotation away from crypto.
summary: A wave of blockbuster AI listings is expected, drawing billions of dollars from global investors and potentially leaving the crypto market further behind. This shift in investment could indicate a rotation away from crypto and towards AI. The upcoming AI IPOs are highly anticipated and may have significant implications for both markets.

title: EU parliament passes ‘chat control,’ allowing private chat scans until 2028
content: EU lawmakers have again allowed tech firms to scan chats for abuse material, but exempted end-to-end encrypted messages.
summary: The EU parliament has passed a law allowing tech firms to scan private chats for abuse material until 2028, while exempting end-to-end encrypted messages. This decision aims to balance online safety with user privacy. The law's implications may impact tech companies and users alike.

title: Robinhood Chain sees over $70M in ETH bridged during first week
content: Robinhood’s choice to use Ethereum further solidifies its position as the “ultimate settlement layer and liquidity foundation for tokenized assets,” said HashKey Group’s Tim Sun.
summary: Robinhood Chain saw over $70M in ETH bridged during its first week, solidifying Ethereum's position as a settlement layer and liquidity foundation. This development is significant for the crypto community. Robinhood's choice to use Ethereum reflects the blockchain's growing importance.

title: Zcash targets July 28 launch for Ironwood network upgrade
content: Zcash’s next upgrade will replace the once-compromised Orchard pool and may offer clues about whether counterfeit tokens were created via a recent bug.
summary: Zcash is planning to launch its Ironwood network upgrade on July 28, replacing the compromised Orchard pool. The upgrade may provide insight into whether counterfeit tokens were created due to a recent bug. This upgrade aims to improve the security of the Zcash network.

title: Hackers tried to backdoor Injective npm package to steal wallet keys
content: The incident is significant for developers and applications that handle Injective wallet workflows, Socket researchers said.
summary: Hackers attempted to backdoor the Injective npm package to gain access to wallet keys, posing a significant risk to developers and applications that handle Injective wallet workflows. Socket researchers discovered the incident, highlighting its importance for the security of wallet workflows. The attempt was ultimately unsuccessful.

title: Phantom, Hyperliquid ask CFTC to modernize rules for onchain derivatives
content: The companies urged the regulator to exempt blockchain developers and non-custodial wallet providers from rules designed for traditional financial intermediaries.
summary: Phantom and Hyperliquid are requesting the CFTC to update its rules for on-chain derivatives, seeking exemptions for blockchain developers and non-custodial wallet providers. This move aims to accommodate the unique characteristics of blockchain technology. The companies want to differentiate these rules from those applied to traditional financial intermediaries.

title: Can Bitcoin hold $62K ahead of Friday’s $1.4 billion options expiry?
content: US 10-year Treasury yields nears dangerous level while major Deribit Bitcoin options expiry approaches.
summary: The Bitcoin price is under scrutiny as a major options expiry approaches on Friday, with $1.4 billion in derivatives set to expire, while US 10-year Treasury yields have neared a dangerous level. This could impact the cryptocurrency market. Bitcoin is currently hovering around $62K

title: MARA shares surge after 2 GW Texas infrastructure deal expands AI ambitions
content: The Bitcoin miner's shares climbed about 15% after announcing it's acquiring a Texas site with up to 2 GW of power capacity to expand its AI and digital infrastructure strategy.
summary: MARA, a Bitcoin miner, has acquired a Texas site with 2 GW of power capacity, expanding its AI and digital infrastructure strategy, resulting in a 15% surge in shares. This deal highlights the growing intersection of cryptocurrency and artificial intelligence. The acquisition aims to support MARA's ambitions in both fields.

title: Bitcoin’s quantum dilemma: Bigger blocks or STARK proofs?
content: Dealing with large post quantum signatures slowing down Bitcoin can be solved with bigger blocks, or aggregating signatures using STARK proofs.
summary: Bitcoin is facing a challenge with large post-quantum signatures slowing down the network, and two possible solutions are being considered: increasing block size or using STARK proofs to aggregate signatures. This dilemma is significant for the future of Bitcoin. The solution chosen will impact the blockchain's security and efficiency.

title: Hong Kong regulator orders new anti-phishing measures for crypto platforms
content: Hong Kong’s regulator has ordered crypto platforms and online brokers to meet newly issued phishing-resistant login requirements within the next 12 months.
summary: Hong Kong's regulator has ordered crypto platforms to implement anti-phishing measures, specifically phishing-resistant login requirements, within 12 months. This move aims to enhance security for users of cryptocurrency platforms. The new requirements are part of the regulator's efforts to protect consumers from phishing attacks.

title: Sony Bank gets US regulator nod to issue stablecoins
content: Sony Bank received preliminary approval from the Office of the Comptroller of the Currency to establish a US stablecoin issuance business, with $40 million in starting capital.
summary: Sony Bank has received preliminary approval to establish a US stablecoin issuance business with $40 million in starting capital. The approval was granted by the Office of the Comptroller of the Currency. This move marks a significant step for Sony Bank in the cryptocurrency space.

title: Interpol operation exposes $122M crypto wallet tied to romance scam laundering
content: Interpol said a suspect's crypto wallet processed over $122.5 million in 10 months as authorities uncovered the scheme during a global anti-fraud operation that led to 5,811 arrests.
summary: Interpol has uncovered a romance scam laundering scheme involving a crypto wallet that processed over $122.5 million in 10 months, resulting in 5,811 arrests globally. The operation was part of a global anti-fraud effort. The suspect's wallet was tied to the scam, which was exposed during the operation.

title: Hyperliquid shows how onchain perps could challenge Wall Street: Pantera
content: Perpetual futures and Hyperliquid’s blockchain infrastructure are expanding into traditional asset classes with around-the-clock trading, according to Pantera Capital.
summary: Pantera Capital believes Hyperliquid's blockchain infrastructure and perpetual futures can challenge Wall Street by expanding into traditional asset classes with 24/7 trading. This could potentially disrupt traditional markets. Hyperliquid's onchain perps may bring new opportunities for traders.

title: Bitcoin ETFs end 'most overwhelming' $2.7B sell-off amid new $85M net outflow
content: Bitcoin spot ETFs posted a fresh net outflow on Wednesday as analysis said that their "most overwhelming" outflow streak had ended without a clear demand recovery.
summary: Bitcoin spot ETFs experienced a significant net outflow of $2.7B, with a new $85M net outflow on Wednesday, ending their most overwhelming outflow streak without a clear demand recovery. The outflows indicate a lack of investor confidence in Bitcoin. The ETFs are still waiting for a demand recovery.

title: Swift launches blockchain ledger with 17-bank tokenized deposit pilot
content: Swift launched its new blockchain ledger that will host a tokenized bank deposit pilot for 17 major banks seeking faster cross-border payments.
summary: SWIFT has launched a blockchain ledger to facilitate faster cross-border payments through a tokenized bank deposit pilot with 17 major banks. This pilot aims to improve the efficiency of international transactions. The use of blockchain technology has the potential to significantly enhance the speed and security of these payments.

title: Regulators invited Binance to seek new licenses after MiCA setback, co-CEO says
content: Binance is exploring new licensing paths into Europe while continuing to expand its regulatory footprint in Asia, says co-CEO Richard Teng.
summary: Binance co-CEO Richard Teng announced the company is seeking new licenses in Europe after a setback with MiCA, while expanding its regulatory presence in Asia. This move indicates Binance's efforts to comply with regulatory requirements and increase its global presence. The company is exploring alternative licensing paths in Europe.

title: Russia's largest private bank Alfa-Bank tests crypto trading
content: Alfa-Bank is testing cryptocurrency trading for qualified investors as Russia moves toward regulating digital assets and banks prepare new crypto services.
summary: Alfa-Bank, Russia's largest private bank, is testing cryptocurrency trading for qualified investors as the country moves towards regulating digital assets. This development indicates a growing acceptance of crypto in Russia's financial sector. The bank's new service is part of a broader trend of banks preparing crypto services.

title: Wyden urges Senate leaders to keep dev protections in crypto bill
content: Senator Ron Wyden has called on Senate leaders to ensure the Blockchain Regulatory Certainty Act remains in the CLARITY Act when it is brought to the floor.
summary: Senator Ron Wyden is urging Senate leaders to preserve developer protections in the crypto bill, specifically the Blockchain Regulatory Certainty Act within the CLARITY Act. This move aims to support the development of the cryptocurrency and blockchain space. Wyden's request highlights the ongoing efforts to regulate the industry.

title: Trader loses $1M after signing phishing token approval
content: Onchain scammers netted more than $14 billion last year, and approval phishing remains a primary attack vector.
summary: A trader lost $1M due to a phishing token approval scam, highlighting the ongoing issue of onchain scams which netted over $14 billion last year. Approval phishing remains a primary attack vector for scammers. This incident serves as a reminder for users to be cautious with their transactions.

title: AI is shortening the shelf life of crypto security audits, researchers warn
content: Hackers have also been exploiting the codebases of defunct decentralized finance protocols, draining millions of dollars in customer funds.
summary: Researchers warn that AI is shortening the shelf life of crypto security audits, making it easier for hackers to exploit vulnerabilities. Hackers have been targeting defunct decentralized finance protocols, draining millions of dollars in customer funds. This highlights a significant security risk in the crypto industry.

title: AI boom fuels inflation fears, complicating Fed’s next rate move
content: Ongoing strong demand for AI infrastructure “would likely sustain upward pressure on prices for technology products and electricity,” Federal Reserve policymakers said.
summary: The AI boom is fueling inflation fears due to strong demand for AI infrastructure, which may lead to sustained upward pressure on technology product and electricity prices. This development complicates the Federal Reserve's next rate move. The situation is being closely monitored by policymakers.

title: Polymarket files applications to offer regulated margin trading in US
content: Polymarket's rival platform, Kalshi, already received US regulatory approval to provide margin trading back in March.
summary: Polymarket has filed applications to offer regulated margin trading in the US, following in the footsteps of its rival Kalshi, which received approval in March. This move aims to provide a regulated platform for users to engage in margin trading. The development is significant for the crypto community.

title: Bitwise says bitcoin’s ‘floor is rising’ despite AI boom and regulatory delays
content: Bitwise says institutions are buying the bitcoin bear market dip, while many larger investors are still awaiting regulatory clarity.
summary: Bitwise reports that institutions are buying into the bitcoin market despite current bear market conditions, with larger investors waiting for regulatory clarity. The company notes that the bitcoin floor is rising, indicating a potential increase in the cryptocurrency's value. This development may suggest growing institutional interest in bitcoin.

title: Ethereum Foundation says AI agents find real bugs, but most are false positives
content: The Ethereum Foundation's Protocol Security team has been using coordinated AI agents to test critical network infrastructure.
summary: The Ethereum Foundation's Protocol Security team is using AI agents to test critical network infrastructure, finding real bugs but mostly false positives. This approach aims to improve the security of the Ethereum network. The use of AI agents helps identify potential vulnerabilities.

title: CryptoQuant says bitcoin rebound remains a bear-market recovery, not a trend reversal
content: CryptoQuant said bitcoin's recent rebound should be viewed as a bear-market recovery rather than a trend reversal.
summary: CryptoQuant believes the recent rebound in bitcoin price is a bear-market recovery, not a sign of a trend reversal. This means the overall downtrend is still intact. The rebound is not expected to lead to a long-term price increase.

title: Hyperliquid Policy Center, Phantom urge CFTC to stop treating onchain protocols like traditional brokers and exchanges
content: The CFTC and Securities and Exchange Commission issued a Request for Information in mid-June on financial innovation.
summary: The CFTC and SEC have issued a Request for Information on financial innovation, prompting Hyperliquid Policy Center and Phantom to urge the CFTC to change its approach to onchain protocols. They argue that onchain protocols should not be treated like traditional brokers and exchanges. This could have significant implications for the crypto industry.

title: JPMorgan says bitcoin’s main risk isn’t Strategy, but blockchain adoption that doesn’t benefit public chains and tokens
content: JPMorgan says Strategy isn't bitcoin's main structural risk, but blockchain adoption that fails to benefit public blockchains and tokens.
summary: JPMorgan states that the main risk to bitcoin is not its strategy, but rather blockchain adoption that does not benefit public blockchains and tokens. This suggests that the bank is concerned about the potential for blockchain technology to be developed in a way that excludes public chains and tokens. As a result, bitcoin's value could be negatively impacted.

title: Supreme Court ruling expanding Trump’s authority over federal agencies raises questions for SEC, CFTC as crypto rulemaking advances
content: A recent Supreme Court ruling expanding President Trump's authority to remove leaders of many agencies could have broader implications.
summary: The Supreme Court has expanded President Trump's authority over federal agencies, raising questions about the impact on the SEC and CFTC as they advance in crypto rulemaking. This ruling could have broader implications for the regulatory landscape of cryptocurrency. The decision may affect the leadership and decision-making processes of these agencies.

title: MARA gains 14% after unveiling 2 GW Texas AI and bitcoin mining campus plan
content: The deal isn't an upfront purchase, but is structured instead as up to $600 million in milestone-based payments
summary: MARA has announced plans for a 2 GW Texas campus that will focus on AI and bitcoin mining, with milestone-based payments of up to $600 million. This project combines artificial intelligence and cryptocurrency mining. The company's stock has risen 14% following the announcement.

title: Hong Kong SFC orders crypto platforms, online brokers to phase out OTP logins
content: Hong Kong SFC orders internet brokers, crypto platforms to replace OTP logins with passkeys within 12 months following 57% spoofing surge.
summary: The Hong Kong SFC has ordered online brokers and crypto platforms to phase out one-time password logins and replace them with passkeys within 12 months. This decision comes after a 57% surge in spoofing incidents. The move aims to enhance security for users of these platforms.

title: PayPal seeks to increase use of PYUSD stablecoin with native issuance on Polygon
content: The company said its stablecoin will now be issued natively on Polygon, in addition to becoming available through Polygon's Open Money Stack.
summary: PayPal's stablecoin, PYUSD, will now be issued natively on Polygon, increasing its availability and use. This move also makes PYUSD available through Polygon's Open Money Stack. The expansion aims to boost the adoption of PYUSD.

title: Tarun Chitra’s Gauntlet raises $125 million Series C from sole investor SBI Holdings
content: Chitra said he expects tokenization and vaults to grow the DeFi market faster than overall stablecoin growth over the next few years.
summary: Gauntlet, a company founded by Tarun Chitra, has raised $125 million in a Series C funding round from sole investor SBI Holdings. Chitra expects tokenization and vaults to drive DeFi market growth, outpacing stablecoin growth in the next few years. This investment may significantly impact the DeFi sector.

title: ‘Bottom building in progress’: Analysts say bitcoin holder capitulation signals late-stage bear market
content: Bitcoin long-term holder capitulation hit $280M/day, the highest since Dec 2022, a sign Glassnode says may flag a bottom forming.
summary: Bitcoin long-term holder capitulation has reached $280M/day, the highest since December 2022, potentially indicating a bottom forming in the market. This signal is seen as a sign of a late-stage bear market. Analysts, including Glassnode, are monitoring the trend to determine if it marks a turning point for the cryptocurrency.

title: Crypto hack losses fall below $1 billion in H1 2026 despite record attack volume: Immunefi
content: Crypto hacks hit a record 207 incidents in H1 2026, but losses fell to $972M as DeFi exploit damage dropped 74% from 2022, per Immunefi.
summary: Crypto hacks reached a record 207 incidents in the first half of 2026, but losses decreased to $972 million due to a 74% drop in DeFi exploit damage. This is according to a report by Immunefi. The decrease in losses is notable despite the high number of incidents.

title: Aave Labs rolls out Stable Vaults, offering predictable stablecoin yield aimed at mainstream users
content: Stable Vaults will continuously “optimize capital allocation” across DeFi yield strategies, like Aave V3 and V4 markets.
summary: Aave Labs has introduced Stable Vaults, a new product that offers predictable stablecoin yields to mainstream users by optimizing capital allocation across various DeFi yield strategies. This product aims to provide a more accessible and stable yield option for users. Stable Vaults will utilize Aave V3 and V4 markets to achieve this.

title: JPMorgan Says the Real Threat to Bitcoin Isn’t Strategy (MSTR) — It’s Private Blockchains
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

JPMorgan Says the Real Threat to Bitcoin Isn’t Strategy (MSTR) — It’s Private Blockchains
JPMorgan says Strategy's Bitcoin sales are a short-term concern, but the bigger long-term risk is that banks and institutions adopt private blockchains over public networks, reducing activity and capital flows across the broader crypto ecosystem.
This post JPMorgan Says the Real Threat to Bitcoin Isn’t Strategy (MSTR) — It’s Private Blockchains first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.
summary: JPMorgan believes the real long-term threat to Bitcoin is not Strategy's Bitcoin sales, but rather the adoption of private blockchains by banks and institutions, which could reduce activity and capital flows in the crypto ecosystem. This could have a significant impact on the broader crypto market. The institution views this as a bigger risk than short-term sales.

title: Bitcoin’s New Debt Machine is Facing Its First Major Test
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

Bitcoin’s New Debt Machine is Facing Its First Major Test
BitcoinTreasuries.net says June marked the first major stress test for Bitcoin-backed preferred shares, with Strategy's STRC and Strive's SATA rebounding after a sharp sell-off, reinforcing confidence in the growing corporate Bitcoin financing model.
This post Bitcoin’s New Debt Machine is Facing Its First Major Test first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.
summary: Bitcoin-backed preferred shares faced their first major stress test in June, with Strategy's STRC and Strive's SATA rebounding after a sharp sell-off, reinforcing confidence in the corporate Bitcoin financing model. This test is significant for the growing Bitcoin financing sector. The outcome has reinforced confidence in the model.

title: New Hampshire Council Rejects $100 Million Bitcoin-Backed Bond
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

New Hampshire Council Rejects $100 Million Bitcoin-Backed Bond
New Hampshire's Executive Council voted 3-2 to reject a proposed $100 million Bitcoin-backed municipal bond, shelving what supporters said would have been the world's first such issuance despite arguments that it posed no risk to taxpayers.
This post New Hampshire Council Rejects $100 Million Bitcoin-Backed Bond first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.
summary: New Hampshire's Executive Council rejected a $100 million Bitcoin-backed municipal bond, which would have been the world's first such issuance. The proposal was voted down 3-2, despite arguments that it posed no risk to taxpayers. The rejection shelves a potentially groundbreaking financial instrument.

title: BitGo Adds Quantum-Risk Controls to Bitcoin Custody
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

BitGo Adds Quantum-Risk Controls to Bitcoin Custody
BitGo launched new quantum-risk tools that help institutional Bitcoin holders identify and reduce potential future quantum-computing exposure in their custody wallets.
This post BitGo Adds Quantum-Risk Controls to Bitcoin Custody first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.
summary: BitGo has launched new tools to help institutional Bitcoin holders identify and reduce potential future quantum-computing exposure in their custody wallets. This move aims to mitigate the risks associated with quantum computing for Bitcoin custody. The tools will help holders protect their assets from potential quantum-related threats.

title: Russia’s Largest Private Bank Alfa-Bank To Test Bitcoin and Crypto Trading
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

Russia’s Largest Private Bank Alfa-Bank To Test Bitcoin and Crypto Trading
Russia's largest private lender, Alfa-Bank, plans to launch Bitcoin and crypto trading, custody, and a digital depository once new regulations take effect, joining a broader push by major Russian banks to enter the country's emerging crypto market by late 2026 or 2027.
This post Russia’s Largest Private Bank Alfa-Bank To Test Bitcoin and Crypto Trading first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.
summary: Alfa-Bank, Russia's largest private lender, plans to launch Bitcoin and crypto trading, custody, and a digital depository once new regulations take effect. This move is part of a broader push by major Russian banks to enter the country's emerging crypto market. The launch is expected to occur by late 2026 or 2027.

title: CFTC Chair Says Clarity Act Is ‘So Close’ As August Deadline Nears
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

CFTC Chair Says Clarity Act Is ‘So Close’ As August Deadline Nears
CFTC Chairman Michael Selig urged Congress to pass the Clarity Act before the August recess, saying the crypto market-structure bill is "so close" despite ongoing disputes over ethics and stablecoin provisions.
This post CFTC Chair Says Clarity Act Is ‘So Close’ As August Deadline Nears first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.
summary: CFTC Chairman Michael Selig is urging Congress to pass the Clarity Act, a crypto market-structure bill, before the August recess, stating it is 'so close' despite ongoing disputes. The bill aims to bring clarity to the crypto market. Its passage is crucial for the crypto community.

title: Bull Bitcoin Files Landmark Legal Challenge to Annul France’s DAC8 Crypto Data Surveillance Rules
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

Bull Bitcoin Files Landmark Legal Challenge to Annul France’s DAC8 Crypto Data Surveillance Rules
The filing marks the first major legal challenge to DAC8 in the EU. Bull Bitcoin warns that forced consolidation of user data undermines both privacy and the regulation’s own tax-collection goals by driving users toward unregulated alternatives.
This post Bull Bitcoin Files Landmark Legal Challenge to Annul France’s DAC8 Crypto Data Surveillance Rules first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Juan Galt.
summary: Bull Bitcoin has filed a landmark legal challenge to annul France's DAC8 crypto data surveillance rules, marking the first major challenge to DAC8 in the EU. The company argues that the rules undermine user privacy and tax-collection goals. This challenge may have significant implications for the crypto community in the EU.

title: Anthropic found a hidden space where Claude puzzles over concepts
content: The AI firm Anthropic has developed a technique that has given it the clearest glimpse yet at what’s really going on inside large language models as they answer questions or carry out tasks. What they found ranges from the mundane to the unnerving. Researchers at the company built a tool called the Jacobian lens (or…
summary: Anthropic developed a technique to better understand large language models, finding a range of results from mundane to unnerving. The tool, called the Jacobian lens, provides insight into the models' thought processes. This discovery could significantly impact AI development.

title: EmTech AI 2026: The Rise of the AI Platform
summary: The EmTech AI 2026 conference discusses the growing importance of AI platforms, highlighting their potential to transform industries and revolutionize the way we live and work. The conference explores the latest advancements in AI technology and its applications. AI platforms are expected to play a crucial role in shaping the future of various sectors.

title: The Download: your stake in OpenAI, and the Treasury’s AI warning
content: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Your family’s $300 stake in OpenAI Sam Altman’s proposal that Americans should share in the wealth created by AI is back in the spotlight, with reports that he is discussing giving…
summary: OpenAI's CEO Sam Altman proposes that Americans should share in the wealth created by AI, with a potential $300 stake for families. This idea is being discussed, sparking interest in the tech community. The Treasury also issued an AI warning, highlighting the significance of AI's impact.

title: The foundational elements of AI architecture that IT leaders need to scale
content: With the rapid progress of AI capabilities and the move to agentic systems, organizations are expanding their use cases as the technology continues to grow. That constant evolution also introduces risk, leaving IT leaders to wonder which investments will prove valuable even six months into the future. Returning to the foundational elements of AI architecture—the…
summary: The article discusses the rapid progress of AI capabilities and the importance of understanding the foundational elements of AI architecture for IT leaders to scale and make valuable investments. It highlights the evolution of AI and the introduction of risk, emphasizing the need for a solid foundation. The article aims to guide IT leaders in navigating the growing technology.

title: Your family’s $300 stake in OpenAI
content: This story originally appeared in The Algorithm, our weekly newsletter on AI. To get stories like this in your inbox first, sign up here. OpenAI CEO Sam Altman’s oft-discussed promise that Americans will share in the wealth AI creates was in the news again last week. On Thursday, the Financial Times reported that Altman is in…
summary: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman has reiterated his promise that Americans will share in the wealth AI creates, with an estimated $300 stake per family, according to a report by the Financial Times. The report suggests that Altman is working on a plan to distribute the wealth generated by AI. This move could have significant implications for the AI community.

title: OpenAI says GPT 5.6 is the ‘preferred model’ for Microsoft Copilot 365 amid breakup chatter
content: OpenAI's new family of models will continue to power Microsoft's suite of workplace and productivity apps.
summary: OpenAI's new models, including GPT 5.6, will continue to power Microsoft's workplace and productivity apps, amid speculation of a potential breakup between the two companies. This ensures continuity for Microsoft's Copilot 365. The partnership remains intact for now.

title: Fidji Simo steps down from OpenAI’s no. 2 role
content: OpenAI's No. 2 executive, Fidji Simo, is stepping down from her full-time role after her medical leave proved longer than expected — a leadership vacuum that comes at a tricky time as the company eyes a possible IPO and races to catch Anthropic in the enterprise market.
summary: Fidji Simo, OpenAI's No. 2 executive, is stepping down from her role after a longer-than-expected medical leave, creating a leadership vacuum at a crucial time for the company. OpenAI is considering an IPO and competing with Anthropic in the enterprise market. This change may impact OpenAI's strategic direction and growth plans.

title: OpenAI launches its new family of models with GPT-5.6
content: OpenAI's latest family of models promises improvements across a range of areas, including cybersecurity.
summary: OpenAI has launched a new family of models, GPT-5.6, which promises improvements in various areas, including cybersecurity. The new models are expected to enhance performance and capabilities. This launch is a significant update from OpenAI.

title: An AI agent startup just let its agent run its $100M fundraise
content: Lyzr, a startup that builds AI agents for enterprises, used its own AI agent to raise a $100 million round — proof, evidently, that the product actually works.
summary: Lyzr, a startup building AI agents, used its own AI agent to raise $100 million, demonstrating the product's effectiveness. This achievement showcases the potential of AI in handling complex tasks, such as fundraising. The successful raise highlights the viability of AI agents in enterprise settings.

title: OpenAI is shutting down Atlas, but its AI browser ambitions are still growing
content: OpenAI is sunsetting its AI-powered browser after less than a year. But it's moving some agentic browsing features to its desktop app and a Chrome extension.
summary: OpenAI is shutting down its AI-powered browser Atlas after less than a year, but will integrate some features into its desktop app and a Chrome extension. This move indicates a shift in OpenAI's browser ambitions. The company is re strategizing its approach to AI-powered browsing.

title: Elon Musk praises Mythos/Fable, promises not to ‘cut off’ Anthropic
content: Should Anthropic trust Elon Musk to host its models? With about $40 billion in revenue at stake, Musk insists that the company can.
summary: Elon Musk has expressed support for Mythos/Fable and promised not to limit Anthropic's access to its models, which could impact the company's $40 billion revenue. This statement aims to reassure Anthropic amidst potential concerns. Musk's assurance is significant for Anthropic's future

title: Can AI answer the $3 trillion question?
content: The AI ROI debate has returned and the numbers are even bigger, as are, perhaps, the consequences.
summary: The AI ROI debate has resurfaced with significantly larger numbers and potential consequences, prompting a discussion on the value of AI with a $3 trillion question. This debate may have substantial implications for the future of AI development and investment. The question is whether AI can provide a worthwhile return on investment.

title: Meta enters the crowded AI coding battle with Muse Spark 1.1
content: Meta's pitch to users is Spark's ability to handle large agentic workloads, fix bugs, and help with large code migrations — the kind of automation that enterprises are increasingly turning to AI companies to provide.
summary: Meta has launched Muse Spark 1.1, an AI coding tool that can handle large workloads, fix bugs, and assist with code migrations, targeting enterprise clients. This move marks Meta's entry into the competitive AI coding market. The tool aims to provide automation solutions that enterprises increasingly need.

title: New York Times says OpenAI hid evidence in ChatGPT copyright trial
content: News publishers say OpenAI hid tools and datasets that could identify copyrighted journalism in ChatGPT outputs, escalating their lawsuit with a new motion for sanctions.
summary: OpenAI is accused of hiding evidence in a copyright trial related to ChatGPT, with news publishers claiming the company concealed tools and datasets that could identify copyrighted journalism in ChatGPT outputs. The lawsuit has been escalated with a new motion for sanctions. This development may have significant implications for AI copyright laws.

title: Google will now disclose which ads are made with AI
content: While Google prohibits misleading and deceptive ads, an ad can still leverage AI to create some type of synthetic or digitally altered content. Until now, that's something Google only required election ads to disclose.
summary: Google will now require advertisers to disclose when their ads use artificial intelligence to create synthetic or digitally altered content, extending a policy previously only applied to election ads. This change aims to increase transparency in online advertising. The move is part of Google's efforts to prevent misleading ads.

title: Paris-based AI voice startup Gradium raises $100M seed, backed by Nvidia
content: The company is using the cash to open an office in the Bay Area and compete for talent there, "strengthening its position at the heart of the world's leading AI ecosystem."
summary: Gradium, a Paris-based AI voice startup, has raised $100M in seed funding backed by Nvidia. The company plans to use the funds to open a new office in the Bay Area and compete for top talent. This move aims to strengthen its position in the global AI ecosystem.

title: How did the government decide OpenAI’s frontier model was safe to release?
content: "Exactly what that dialog looked like between the government and Anthropic and OpenAI is unclear."
summary: The government's decision to release OpenAI's frontier model is unclear, with details of the dialogue between the government and OpenAI and Anthropic not being disclosed. The decision-making process behind this release is still shrouded in mystery. This has raised questions about the safety and implications of releasing such models.

title: Meta’s new AI chips will begin production in September
content: The company is taking a modular approach to designing these chips, anticipating that their needs will change as AI evolves rapidly by the time the chips are in production.
summary: Meta will begin production of its new AI-specific chips in September, aiming to reduce reliance on GPU providers like Nvidia. This move is part of Meta's efforts to optimize its AI infrastructure. The new chips are designed to improve AI processing efficiency.

title: Nvidia is a victim of the compute marketplace it created
content: Having proven how valuable compute can be, the company finds itself at the center of a market everyone wants to be in — while simpler technologies and less interesting companies get rich on the sidelines.
summary: Nvidia created a valuable compute marketplace, but now finds itself at the center of a competitive market where others are profiting from simpler technologies. The company's success has attracted many players to the market. Nvidia is now facing competition from less interesting companies.

title: Anthropic’s new Claude feature is quietly selling you on AI
content: Claude’s new Reflect dashboard doesn’t just visualize how you use AI. It also subtly reinforces how much of your daily work now depends on Anthropic’s chatbot.
summary: Anthropic's new Claude feature includes a Reflect dashboard that visualizes AI usage and reinforces dependence on the chatbot. This subtle reinforcement showcases how much daily work relies on Anthropic's AI. The feature aims to sell users on the benefits of AI integration.

title: Popular open source AI developer tool Ollama raises $65M, grows to nearly 9M users
content: Benchmark-backed Ollama has amassed 176,000 stars, and nearly 17,000 forks on GitHub by helping developers easily run AI on their PCs.
summary: Ollama, a popular open source AI developer tool, has raised $65M and grown to nearly 9M users, with 176,000 stars and 17,000 forks on GitHub. It helps developers easily run AI on their PCs. This tool has gained significant traction in the AI development community.

title: Character.AI enters the microdrama arena with its own productions, but there’s a twist
content: In an interesting twist that takes advantage of the company's core product, users can chat with these shows' characters, ask them questions, and even roleplay different storylines.
summary: Character.AI is producing its own microdramas, allowing users to interact with characters, ask questions, and roleplay storylines. This innovation leverages the company's core product, enabling unique engagement with the shows. The platform combines entertainment and artificial intelligence for immersive experiences.

title: Fidji Simo steps down from leading OpenAI’s AGI work due to illness
content:
OpenAI's Fidji Simo is departing her full-time role as the company's AGI chief and is transitioning to being a "part-time advisor," she said on X.
The news follows Simo's original announcement in April that she would take a few weeks of medical leave due to a neuroimmune condition, shortly after she had taken on the AGI chief title. (She had formerly been the company's CEO of applications). Around the same time, COO Brad Lightcap also stepped down from his role to focus on "special projects," and OpenAI CMO Kate Rouch also stepped down in order to focus on her health. Rouch planned to return to a "more narrowly scoped role" when her health  …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: Fidji Simo, OpenAI's AGI chief, is stepping down due to a neuroimmune condition and will transition to a part-time advisor role. She had taken on the AGI chief title after previously serving as CEO of applications. The move follows other recent departures from OpenAI, including COO Brad Lightcap and CMO Kate Rouch.

title: Google will now tell you if an ad was made with AI
content:
You can see if ads on Google Search, Google Discover, and YouTube were made or edited using AI from a new section in Google's "My Ad Center," as reported earlier by TechCrunch. The update, announced on Thursday, adds a "created or edited with AI" label under the "how this ad was made" tab. Users can find it by tapping the three dots or info button on ads, which pulls up the same panel where you can also block or report ads.
Google says it will automatically apply the AI label to any ads made with its own generative AI advertising tools, but AI ads made elsewhere will need to have the label applied manually. In some regions, Google's new AI  …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: Google has introduced a new feature that labels ads created or edited with AI, visible in the 'My Ad Center' section on Google Search, Google Discover, and YouTube. The label will be automatically applied to ads made with Google's generative AI tools, while others will require manual labeling. This update aims to increase transparency in ad creation.

title: Microsoft’s patch Tuesdays are about to get bigger
content:
Windows 11 updates could soon include fixes for more security issues at once. Microsoft said in a blog post on Thursday that it's now using AI to "identify potential issues earlier," which means "customers will see a higher volume of security updates included in each security release." 
Hackers, even amateurs, have increasingly been using AI to quickly exploit security weaknesses over the past several months. Security researchers are also using AI to find issues faster, leading to more frequent high-severity vulnerabilities, like the "Copy Fail" exploit that impacted nearly every Linux distribution in May. Similarly, when Anthropic announce …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: Microsoft is using AI to identify potential security issues earlier, resulting in a higher volume of security updates in each release, as hackers and security researchers increasingly use AI to exploit and find vulnerabilities. This is in response to the growing use of AI by hackers to quickly exploit security weaknesses. The move aims to improve Windows 11 security.

title: OpenAI rolls out GPT-5.6 after government greenlight — and announces ‘ChatGPT Work’
content:
About two weeks after OpenAI's GPT-5.6 was caught up in regulatory drama - rolled out only to government-approved organizations during a "limited preview" period - the company has received the Trump administration's greenlight for a public rollout of the model. OpenAI CEO Sam Altman called it "the best model we have ever produced."
To celebrate, OpenAI also unveiled a new AI agent on the same day: ChatGPT Work. It's billed as a combination of ChatGPT and Codex, allowing the everyday non-technical user to take advantage of Codex's capabilities for non-coding tasks, and it's powered by the GPT-5.6 model suite (Sol, Terra, and Luna). "It can g …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: OpenAI has rolled out its new GPT-5.6 model after receiving government approval, with CEO Sam Altman calling it the company's best model yet. The rollout was previously limited to government-approved organizations. OpenAI also unveiled ChatGPT Work, a new AI agent that combines ChatGPT and Codex.

title: Meta says its new AI model is ready to compete on coding
content:
After reentering the AI race with its first in-house Muse Spark model in April, Meta is now opening up the doors to developers with a new model that can plug into AI coding software with the new Meta Model API.
Meta says that Muse Spark 1.1 is a "step-change" from the first generation, with improvements based on feedback from developers. The company says it's capable of more advanced coding, including detection and fixing of complex bugs; better supports end-to-end agentic workflows across a range of apps, including multi-agent systems; and has native multimodal perception across images, videos, and documents.
The Muse Spark 1.1 launch fol …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: Meta has released its new AI model, Muse Spark 1.1, which can plug into AI coding software with the Meta Model API, and offers improved coding capabilities, including bug detection and fixing. The model is a significant upgrade from the first generation, with advancements in multimodal perception and end-to-end agentic workflows. This launch marks a major step for Meta in the AI race.

title: Character.AI wants a piece of the microdrama pie
content:
Character.AI's plan to become more than just an LLM-powered chatbot platform is going beyond interactive books, comics, and audio dramas. Today, the company announced the debut of c.ai Series - short-form, episodic videos designed to be watched and interacted with - on your phone. 
Unlike traditional microdrama services that feature cheaply produced, live-action shows starring human performers, c.ai Series are animated and almost entirely made with generative AI. The company's interest in getting into the microdrama space isn't surprising considering that it's projected to become a $26 billion industry in the next few years. And even though …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: Character.AI is expanding its platform beyond interactive books and comics with the launch of c.ai Series, short-form animated videos created with generative AI. The company is entering the microdrama space, which is projected to become a $26 billion industry. Character.AI's use of AI-powered animation sets it apart from traditional microdrama services.

title: FL Studio 2026 turns its AI chatbot into your assistant engineer
content:
Gopher can’t play the piano for you, but it can bury it in the mix. | Image: Image Line	

Last year, Image Line introduced Gopher for FL Studio, an AI chatbot that was basically a glorified instruction manual. You asked it how to do something, and it would serve up the relevant instructions. It's the kind of thing I actually use AI for on a semi-regular basis. But in the new release, Gopher can actually execute actions on your behalf. I was able to ask Gopher to lay down a four-on-the-floor kick, with snares on the backbeat, then add a gated reverb on the snare for that '80s pomp, and it executed the instructions flawlessly. 
Gopher does have some limitations. It can't create and draw automation for you. It can't insert notes or …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: FL Studio 2026 has updated its AI chatbot, Gopher, to execute actions on behalf of the user, such as laying down a kick drum and adding gated reverb. Gopher can now assist with tasks, but has limitations, like not being able to create automation or insert notes. This update makes Gopher a more useful tool for musicians and producers.

title: OpenAI wants its new tool to do your work for you and with you
content: Rebranded Codex promises independent workflows that can run "for hours if needed."
summary: OpenAI's new tool, rebranded Codex, aims to assist and automate workflows, allowing for independent operation for extended periods. This advancement has the potential to significantly impact productivity. Codex can run for hours if needed, making it a valuable asset for various tasks.

title: Humanoid robots controlled by surgeons did world-first operation on live pigs
content: Preclinical trial is testing the feasibility of humanoid robots in surgery.
summary: A preclinical trial is testing the feasibility of using humanoid robots controlled by surgeons to perform operations, with a world-first operation being conducted on live pigs. This trial aims to assess the potential of humanoid robots in surgery. The use of robots in surgery could potentially revolutionize the field.

title: OpenAI may have made a fatal misstep in copyright fight with news orgs
content: OpenAI may be sanctioned for hiding, deleting ChatGPT logs in NYT copyright fight.
summary: OpenAI is facing potential sanctions for allegedly hiding and deleting ChatGPT logs in a copyright dispute with the New York Times. The case could have significant implications for AI companies and their handling of user data. OpenAI's actions may be seen as a misstep in its legal battle with news organizations.

title: Suspecting AI cheating, Ivy League prof ordered an in-person final; scores fell 50%
content: AI cheating leads to "a failed society," professor says.
summary: An Ivy League professor suspected AI cheating and ordered an in-person final, which resulted in scores falling by 50%. The professor believes AI cheating can lead to a failed society. This highlights the growing concern of AI-assisted cheating in academic settings.

title: Lawsuit: Man used Grok to make 7K sex images of stepdaughter, then shot himself
content: More young girls sue X over Grok CSAM; X accused of shielding child predators.
summary: A man allegedly used AI software Grok to create explicit images of his stepdaughter, and later took his own life. This incident is part of a larger lawsuit against the company behind Grok, accused of enabling child predators. Multiple young girls are suing the company for its handling of child sexual abuse material.

title: Hackers can use 9 of the most popular AI tools to assemble massive botnets
content: "HalluSquatting" weaponizes LLMs' inability to say "I don't know."
summary: Hackers can utilize 9 popular AI tools to create massive botnets by exploiting the limitations of large language models, specifically their inability to respond with uncertainty. This vulnerability, known as HalluSquatting, allows attackers to assemble and control botnets. The discovery highlights potential security risks associated with AI tools.

title: Facing US export controls, China's DeepSeek plans to make its own chips
content: It's early, but the plan is to reduce dependency on Nvidia and Huawei.
summary: China's DeepSeek is planning to develop its own chips to reduce dependence on Nvidia and Huawei due to US export controls. This move is still in its early stages. The goal is to decrease reliance on foreign technology.

title: How AI could enable autonomous robot workers in workplaces—and maybe homes
content: Top robotics researchers and founders explain how robot autonomy is evolving.
summary: Top robotics researchers and founders discuss the evolution of robot autonomy, which could lead to autonomous robot workers in workplaces and homes. This development is made possible by advancements in artificial intelligence. The technology has the potential to significantly impact various industries and daily life.

title: Secret Claude tracker shocks users after Anthropic’s anti-surveillance stance
content: Anthropic accused of spying on users; engineer says “experiment” is over.
summary: Anthropic, a company known for its AI technology, has been accused of spying on users through a secret tracker in its Claude tool, contradicting its anti-surveillance stance. An engineer stated that the experiment is now over. This incident raises concerns about user privacy and trust in AI companies.

title: UK regulator warns of "arms race" to keep up with AI use in financial services
content: FCA official makes case for greater powers for watchdog as millions use technology for personal finance decisions.
summary: A UK regulator has warned of an 'arms race' to keep up with the increasing use of AI in financial services, with millions using the technology for personal finance decisions. The regulator is calling for greater powers to oversee the industry. This warning highlights the growing importance of AI in finance.

title: After spooking Trump into safety testing, Anthropic AI models get global release
content: US lifts curbs on Anthropic’s advanced Fable and Mythos models.
summary: The US has lifted restrictions on Anthropic's advanced AI models, Fable and Mythos, permitting their global release. This follows earlier safety testing prompted by concerns raised by former President Trump.

title: Live markets: Bitcoin ETFs bleed again while ether funds snap a five-day inflow streak
content: Spot bitcoin funds lost about $95 million on Thursday and ether funds roughly $52 million, ending the one bright spot in crypto's institutional flows even as prices rallied.
summary: On Thursday, spot Bitcoin funds experienced outflows of approximately $95 million, while ether funds saw roughly $52 million in outflows, ending their five-day inflow streak. These significant institutional outflows occurred despite a general rally in cryptocurrency prices.

title: New Hampshire snuffs out trailblazing state-government bitcoin bond effort
content: At its last stage for government approval, the state's executive council rejected the bond project 3-2.
summary: New Hampshire's executive council rejected a pioneering state-government bitcoin bond project in a 3-2 vote. This decision, made at its last stage for government approval, effectively snuffs out the effort to integrate cryptocurrency into state financial operations.

title: With SEC fight over, Coinbase's top legal exec Grewal moves on, and others reassigned
content: Coinbase Chief Legal Officer Paul Grewal will depart the exchange, though he'll continue as an adviser, he said Thursday.
summary: Coinbase's Chief Legal Officer, Paul Grewal, is departing from his executive role at the exchange. He will, however, remain with the company as an advisor.

title: Age verification is the surveillance nobody voted for
content: KOSA and Chat Control both retreated on their most-criticized measures this week, but both kept mandatory age verification, the quiet mechanism that turns anonymous browsing into identified browsing for all of us, argues Billions CEO Evin McMullen.
summary: KOSA and Chat Control bills recently removed some controversial measures but retained mandatory age verification. Billions CEO Evin McMullen argues this mechanism is a form of surveillance, effectively turning anonymous online activity into identified browsing for all users.

title: Crypto remains resilient in face of renewed Middle East tensions
content: BTC rose 1.2% to $63,000 and Nasdaq futures jumped 2.6% with markets seemingly unconcerned by U.S. airstrikes on Iran. Bitcoin is now up 9% since the end of June.
summary: Bitcoin (BTC) showed resilience, rising 1.2% to $63,000 despite renewed Middle East tensions and U.S. airstrikes on Iran. This pushed Bitcoin's gains to 9% since late June, indicating that broader markets appear unfazed by geopolitical events.

title: Bitcoin miners’ AI pivot faces investor scrutiny over insider sales
content: As AI-driven mining stocks retreat, investors are examining executive stock sales, governance and shareholder alignment across leading Bitcoin miners, according to Blocksbridge Consulting.
summary: Investors are scrutinizing executive stock sales, governance, and shareholder alignment among leading Bitcoin miners. This scrutiny arises as these companies, which have pivoted towards AI-driven mining, see their stocks retreat.

title: Trump gets OpenAI to offer US 5% stake, far lower than Sanders’ target
content: Insiders say Sam Altman is in active talks with the Trump administration.
summary: Insiders report that Sam Altman is in active talks with the Trump administration about OpenAI offering a 5% stake in the United States, a figure that is much lower than the target suggested by Senator Sanders. The move highlights potential political involvement and investment discussions surrounding the AI firm.

title: Google’s AI buildout drove 37% increase in electricity use in 2025
content: Google tries balancing AI data center emissions with clean energy efforts.
summary: Google's rapid expansion of AI workloads is projected to raise its electricity consumption by 37% in 2025. The company is attempting to offset the resulting emissions by increasing its use of clean energy sources in its data centers.

title: New attack provides one more reason why AI browsers are a bad idea
content: Telling an LLM that 2 + 2 = 5 is enough to make it follow forbidden instructions.
summary: Researchers have demonstrated a new jailbreak attack where simply convincing a large language model that 2 + 2 = 5 causes it to execute prohibited instructions, highlighting another drawback of AI-driven browsers.

title: XRP up 2% as buyers push through $1.10 resistance
content: A late-session volume surge carried XRP above its recent range, with traders now watching whether $1.10 can hold as support.
summary: XRP's price has increased by 2% after buyers pushed through the $1.10 resistance level, with traders now monitoring if this level can hold as support. The late-session volume surge led to this price movement. The cryptocurrency's next steps are being closely watched by traders.

title: Bitcoin's quiet split: Strong in USD, lagging in JPY as Yen rises on intervention fears
content: A sharp rise in the yen has left bitcoin and other major cryptocurrencies underperforming in yen terms compared with their dollar-based trading pairs.
summary: Bitcoin is performing strongly in USD but lagging in JPY due to a sharp rise in the yen. This disparity is caused by intervention fears, affecting Bitcoin's value in yen terms. The difference highlights the impact of currency fluctuations on cryptocurrency trading pairs.

title: Bitcoin's dwindling exchange reserves don't pack the same bullish punch anymore
content: Santiment reported that bitcoin supply is at its lowest since 2017 and ether since 2015, adding that it doesn’t guarantee prices will move higher but helps set up crypto’s next bull cycle.
summary: Bitcoin's exchange reserves are at their lowest since 2017, and ether's reserves are at their lowest since 2015, according to Santiment. This development may contribute to the next bull cycle, but it doesn't guarantee higher prices. The dwindling reserves were once considered a bullish sign, but their impact has lessened over time.

title: DeFi may be ‘quietly re-rating’ given outperformance against Bitcoin: Bitwise
content: “DeFi usually swings much harder than Bitcoin, so holding up this well is unusual,” says Bitwise, as DeFi tokens have outperformed Bitcoin.
summary: DeFi tokens have outperformed Bitcoin, with Bitwise noting this is unusual as DeFi typically experiences more volatility than Bitcoin. This outperformance may indicate a 'quietly re-rating' of DeFi. Bitwise finds this trend noteworthy given the usual price movements of DeFi and Bitcoin.

title: Coinbase chief legal officer to transition to advisory role on July 31
content: The soon-to-be former chief legal officer did not announce the details of his next move but said that he would continue working with the Board of Coinbase National Trust Company.
summary: Coinbase's chief legal officer will transition to an advisory role on July 31, and will continue working with the Board of Coinbase National Trust Company. The details of his next move have not been announced. This change may impact Coinbase's legal and regulatory strategies.

title: Bitdeer stock jumps 14% as company expands US mining hardware production
content: Bitdeer shares rallied after the company announced a $36 million Nevada manufacturing facility that will produce its SEALMINER Bitcoin mining machines and expand its hardware business.
summary: Bitdeer's stock price increased by 14% after announcing a new $36 million manufacturing facility in Nevada to produce Bitcoin mining hardware. The facility will expand the company's hardware business and produce SEALMINER Bitcoin mining machines. This move aims to increase Bitdeer's presence in the US mining hardware market.

title: UK politicians mull permanent crypto donation ban in wake of Nigel Farage scandal
content: Labour lawmakers proposed a full moratorium on crypto donations in March, which some have sought be made permanent after millions of dollars in contributions were linked to Reform leader Nigel Farage.
summary: UK Labour lawmakers have proposed a ban on crypto donations after millions of dollars in contributions were linked to Reform leader Nigel Farage, with some seeking to make the ban permanent. This comes after a scandal involving Farage and crypto donations. The proposal aims to prevent undue influence from cryptocurrency donations.

title: Bitcoin traders reveal key levels as BTC price passes $63K after Trump Iran 'deal' comments
content: Bitcoin gained new upside targets for the daily close and beyond after US president Donald Trump said that Iran "wants to make a deal."
summary: Bitcoin's price surged past $63K after US President Donald Trump commented on a potential Iran deal, providing new upside targets. Trump's statement sparked a positive reaction in the market. The increase in Bitcoin's price has garnered attention from traders.

title: Revolut says USDT delisting is limited to EEA, Switzerland
content: Revolut said USDT support remains unchanged outside EEA and Switzerland as it winds down the stablecoin offering in selected European markets.
summary: Revolut is delisting USDT in the European Economic Area and Switzerland, but USDT support remains unchanged in other regions. This move is part of Revolut's decision to wind down the stablecoin offering in selected European markets. The delisting is limited to these areas.

title: Bank of Korea stands firm on bank-led stablecoin push as deposit token pilots advance
content: The Bank of Korea reiterated its call for bank-led won stablecoin issuance while advancing deposit token pilots, as issuer rules remain a sticking point in South Korea’s digital asset bill.
summary: The Bank of Korea is pushing for bank-led stablecoin issuance and advancing deposit token pilots, amid debates over issuer rules in South Korea's digital asset bill. This move aims to promote the use of stablecoins in the country. The bank's stance on stablecoin issuance remains firm.

title: North Carolina passes bill recognizing CFTC preemption over prediction markets
content: North Carolina's new law taxes prediction market platforms at 6% of their net trading fee revenue that is attributable to North Carolina residents.
summary: North Carolina has passed a bill that recognizes the Commodity Futures Trading Commission's (CFTC) preemption over prediction markets and imposes a 6% tax on prediction market platforms' net trading fee revenue from state residents. This law affects how prediction markets operate in the state. The move may have implications for the broader regulatory environment of prediction markets.

title: Coinbase Chief Legal Officer Paul Grewal steps down, remains in advisory role
content: Coinbase Chief Legal Officer Paul Grewal joined the firm in mid-2020 to lead its legal, compliance and government relations operations.
summary: Coinbase Chief Legal Officer Paul Grewal has stepped down from his role, but will remain in an advisory capacity. He joined the company in 2020 to lead legal, compliance, and government relations. This change may impact Coinbase's operations and regulatory affairs.

title: White House defends Trump’s regulatory appointments as CFTC vacancies complicate crypto bill push
content: The White House pushed back against Democrats’ criticism that the administration has declined to appoint commissioners to regulatory agencies.
summary: The White House is defending its record on appointing commissioners to regulatory agencies amidst criticism from Democrats. These vacancies, particularly at the CFTC, are reportedly complicating efforts to advance cryptocurrency legislation.

title: Why this Wall Street veteran is betting on Avalanche
content: Avalanche Treasury Company CEO Bart Smith explains his beliefs on the functions of treasury companies and his bull thesis for Avalanche.
summary: Avalanche Treasury Company CEO Bart Smith discusses his positive outlook on Avalanche, sharing his beliefs on treasury companies and their functions. He presents a bull thesis for Avalanche, indicating his confidence in the project's potential. Smith's views are noteworthy given his Wall Street veteran status.

title: Stripe-owned Privy and Jito co-develop Solana transaction inclusion tool FullSend
content: FullSend works by automatically routing every transaction signed in a Privy wallet directly to the current Solana block-building leader.
summary: Privy and Jito have co-developed FullSend, a Solana transaction inclusion tool that automatically routes transactions to the current Solana block-building leader. This tool aims to improve transaction efficiency on the Solana network. The development is a result of collaboration between Stripe-owned Privy and Jito.

title: Bitcoin Slips to $62,000, Paring Rebound as CryptoQuant Sees Room Higher
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

Bitcoin Slips to $62,000, Paring Rebound as CryptoQuant Sees Room Higher
Bitcoin slipped to around $62,000 after rebounding from last week's $57,700 low, while CryptoQuant says improving demand, seasonality and valuation support further gains, though its bearish Bull Score Index suggests the move remains a bear-market recovery rather than a full trend reversal.
This post Bitcoin Slips to $62,000, Paring Rebound as CryptoQuant Sees Room Higher first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.
summary: Bitcoin's price slipped to $62,000 after a rebound from $57,700, with CryptoQuant suggesting potential for further gains due to improving demand and seasonality. However, the Bull Score Index indicates this may be a bear-market recovery rather than a trend reversal. The cryptocurrency's valuation is seen as supportive of higher prices.

title: Schwab Strategist Backs Strategy’s STRC Playbook Amid Bitcoin Weakness
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

Schwab Strategist Backs Strategy’s STRC Playbook Amid Bitcoin Weakness
Schwab strategist Jim Ferraioli said Strategy's recent preferred stock actions have eased near-term liquidity concerns despite Bitcoin's decline.
This post Schwab Strategist Backs Strategy’s STRC Playbook Amid Bitcoin Weakness first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.
summary: Schwab strategist Jim Ferraioli supports Strategy's STRC Playbook, citing eased near-term liquidity concerns despite Bitcoin's decline. This comes as Bitcoin has been experiencing weakness. The strategist's backing is a notable development in the crypto space.

title: Cantor SPAC and Adam Back’s Bitcoin Treasury Renegotiate Merger Terms, Vow New Structure
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

Cantor SPAC and Adam Back’s Bitcoin Treasury Renegotiate Merger Terms, Vow New Structure
Cantor-backed SPAC CEPO and Adam Back's Bitcoin Standard Treasury Company scrapped the original terms of their planned merger and will negotiate a revised structure designed to better reflect current market conditions.
This post Cantor SPAC and Adam Back’s Bitcoin Treasury Renegotiate Merger Terms, Vow New Structure first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.
summary: Cantor-backed SPAC CEPO and Adam Back's Bitcoin Standard Treasury Company are renegotiating their merger terms due to current market conditions. They will create a new structure that better reflects the market. The original terms of the planned merger have been scrapped.

title: Instagram users: Here’s how to stop Meta’s AI from using your photos
content: Muse Image allows users to generate AI images using photos from public Instagram accounts. As long as a person's profile is public, another user can tag that account and use their images as part of an AI-generated creation.
summary: Instagram users can stop Meta's AI from using their photos by changing their account settings to private, as Meta's AI can use photos from public accounts to generate AI images. This is done through a feature called Muse Image, which allows users to create AI-generated images using public photos. Users can protect their photos by making their account private.

title: Nandan Nilekani leaves GP role at Fundamentum as it launches $200M third fund
content: Nilekani remains Fundamentum's anchor investor as the firm expands its leadership team and targets AI and fintech startups in India.
summary: Nandan Nilekani has stepped down as GP at Fundamentum, but remains an anchor investor as the firm launches its third fund, focusing on AI and fintech startups in India. The new fund is worth $200M. Fundamentum is expanding its leadership team.

title: The ChatGPT browser is already dead
content:
OpenAI is already shutting down ChatGPT Atlas, its browser that could do tasks for you on your behalf, less than a year after launching it. Atlas was announced in October, but as part of its wave of news about ChatGPT Work today, the company confirmed that it will be "sunsetting" Atlas and is targeting an August 9th date for deprecation.
The shutdown follows OpenAI's push to reduce "side quests" and catch up with Anthropic on productivity features. As part of that push, in March, The Wall Street Journal reported that OpenAI planned to combine the ChatGPT app, Codex, and Atlas into a desktop "superapp" - ChatGPT Work appears to be the result …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: OpenAI is shutting down ChatGPT Atlas, a browser that could perform tasks on behalf of users, less than a year after its launch. The decision is part of OpenAI's effort to reduce side projects and focus on productivity features. ChatGPT Atlas will be deprecated on August 9th.

title: Say hello to Claude Wrapped
content:
The popularity of Spotify Wrapped has kicked off a wide range of year-in-review features, on apps from YouTube to Uber - and now, the lookback trend has come to AI. Anthropic on Thursday announced a "reflect" feature for its Claude chatbot, allowing users to see an analysis of their usage data over the past month, three months, six months, or year. 
Anthropic bills the reflection dashboard as a way to "see your patterns and shape them," the company wrote in a blog post. It begins with a summary of an individual's key topics brought up with Claude, as well as types of tasks they delegate and their usage patterns, including peak usage times.  …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: Anthropic's Claude chatbot has introduced a new 'reflect' feature, allowing users to analyze their usage data over various time periods, providing insights into their patterns and interactions with the AI. The feature aims to help users understand and shape their behavior. The introduction of this feature follows the popularity of year-in-review features on other apps.

title: Google updates Android Bench with new LLMs, but Gemini still lags behind
content: Android Bench is evolving, and developers can help guide that process.
summary: Google has updated Android Bench with new large language models (LLMs), but Gemini still lags behind. This update allows developers to contribute to the evolution of Android Bench. The introduction of new LLMs aims to improve performance and efficiency.

title: Musk’s X poses “serious risk to Americans’ privacy,” advocates warn FTC
content: FTC urged to reject Elon Musk’s bid to end X monitoring amid AI concerns.
summary: Advocates warn that Elon Musk's plan to stop monitoring content on X could pose a serious risk to the privacy of American users, especially given the platform's use of artificial intelligence for data analysis. They urge the FTC to reject the bid, emphasizing the need for stronger privacy protections.

title: Google's new Nano Banana 2 Lite image model is its fastest and cheapest yet
content: They may not look as good, but Nano Banana 2 Lite images only take a few seconds to create.
summary: Google has unveiled the Nano Banana 2 Lite, an image generation model that is the fastest and cheapest in its series. It can create images in just a few seconds, though the visual quality is lower than more advanced models.

title: Crypto trader applies legendary HODL strategy to EUR/USD forex bet
content: A trader on Ostium has held a $1.14 million long position in EUR/USD perpetual futures for 400 days, applying a bitcoin-style HODLing strategy to forex.
summary: A trader on Ostium has adopted a bitcoin-style HODLing strategy for a traditional forex bet, holding a $1.14 million long position in EUR/USD perpetual futures for 400 days.

title: Trump's plan to redesign every .gov website leads to AI-designed horrors
content: A year in, National Design Studio delays plan to update government web standards.
summary: The National Design Studio has delayed its plan to overhaul .gov website standards originally proposed under Trump, citing challenges that have resulted in AI-generated design proposals that are considered problematic. The delay highlights concerns over the use of AI in public web design.
